questionHtml: Explain, using two equations, how an equimolar solution of ammonia and ammonium ions acts as a buffer solution when small amounts of acid or base are added.
markschemeHtml:
NH3 (aq) + H+ (aq) → NH4 + (aq)    [✔]

NH4 + (aq) + OH− (aq) → NH3 (aq) + H2O(l)    [✔]

 

Note: Accept reaction arrows or equilibrium signs in both equations.

Award [1 max], based on two correct reverse equations but not clearly showing reacting with acid or base but rather dissociation.

meta.title: Explain, using two equations, how an equimolar solution of ammonia and ammonium ions acts as a buffer solution when small amounts of acid or base are added.
